summary: SKCanvas.DrawText ignores SKPaint.IsAntialias property since version 3.116.0 — setting IsAntialias to false no longer disables text antialiasing, though directly setting SKFont.Edging to Alias works as a workaround.

bugSignals.errorMessage: SKPaint.IsAntialias has no effect on DrawText — text remains antialiased regardless of the property value

stepsToReproduce: Create an SKPaint with IsAntialias = false and a TextSize set
Observe that text is rendered with antialiasing (smoothed edges) instead of aliased (jagged/pixelated)
Compare with SkiaSharp 2.88.9 where the same code produces correctly aliased text

repoLinks.description: Minimal repro project demonstrating broken and working versions

screenshots.description: Broken (3.116.x): IsAntialias=false still shows smoothed text
Broken (3.116.x): IsAntialias=true looks similar to false

versionAnalysis.relevanceReason: Confirmed still broken in 3.119.0 by a commenter (jswolf19, July 2025). The SkCompatPaint updateFontEdging mechanism exists but appears ineffective.

regression.reason: Reporter provides screenshots showing correct behavior in pre-3.116.0 and broken behavior in 3.116.0+. A commenter confirms still broken in 3.119.0. The SkCompatPaint bridge layer was designed to preserve this behavior (PR #1802) but the edging update appears ineffective in text blob rendering.

analysis.summary: SKPaint.IsAntialias no longer controls text antialiasing in DrawText. The SkCompatPaint layer (added in PR #1802) correctly calls updateFontEdging() when setAntiAlias() is called, updating the embedded font's Edging to kAlias. However, the visual result in 3.116.0+ is incorrect — text appears antialiased regardless. Setting SKFont.Edging directly to Alias works, suggesting the issue is in how the embedded font's edging propagates through text blob creation or the rendering pipeline.
analysis.rationale: Bug classification with high confidence: reporter provides side-by-side screenshots showing clear visual regression, a minimal repro project, and a community member confirms the issue persists in 3.119.0. The SkCompatPaint::updateFontEdging() mechanism is designed to handle exactly this case (PR #1802), but something in the chain between font edging update and text rendering is broken. This is area/SkiaSharp (not Views) because the issue is in core SKCanvas.DrawText + SKPaint interaction.

keySignals.text: Prior to 3.116.0, setting this property to false produces [aliased text]
The SKPaint.IsAntialias property seems to have no effect on the SKCanvas.DrawText method
keySignals.interpretation: Both true and false produce similar antialiased-looking output

keySignals.text: Setting SKFont.Edging to SKFontEdging.Alias appears to have the desired effect
keySignals.interpretation: Direct SKFont.Edging control works — the issue is in the bridge from SKPaint.IsAntialias to SKFont.Edging, not in the rendering pipeline itself

keySignals.text: This is still an issue in 3.119.0
keySignals.interpretation: Not yet fixed in latest version

codeInvestigation.finding: IsAntialias setter calls sk_compatpaint_set_is_antialias (correct compat function). Getter calls sk_paint_is_antialias (reads base SkPaint flag). The C# bindings are correctly wired.

setAntiAlias() calls SkPaint::setAntiAlias(aa) then updateFontEdging(). updateFontEdging() reads isAntiAlias() and sets fFont.setEdging() to kAlias, kAntiAlias, or kSubpixelAntiAlias. This chain is correct in theory.
sk_compatpaint_get_font() returns pointer to internal fFont (no copy). The C# SKFont wrapper should reflect edging changes made by updateFontEdging().

DrawText(string, float, float, SKPaint) calls paint.GetFont() to get the embedded font, then creates SKTextBlob.Create(text, font) and draws it. The font should carry the correct edging from updateFontEdging().

workarounds: Set SKFont.Edging = SKFontEdging.Alias directly instead of relying on SKPaint.IsAntialias to control text antialiasing
nextQuestions: Does the text blob creation (allocRun) properly preserve the font's edging property in current Skia?
Is there a Skia version change that causes font edging to be overridden during text rendering?
Is the issue platform-independent or Windows-specific? (#1996 reports same on Linux)
Does the C# SKFont caching (??= pattern in GetFont) cause a stale state when IsAntialias is toggled?

resolution.hypothesis: The SkCompatPaint.updateFontEdging() correctly sets the embedded font's edging, but somewhere between the font edging update and text blob rendering, the edging value is lost or overridden. Possible causes: (1) the text blob builder copies font data but drops/resets edging, (2) a newer Skia version handles kAlias differently, or (3) the C# GetFont() caching returns a wrapper that doesn't reflect native-side edging changes.

proposals.title: Use SKFont.Edging directly
proposals.description: Instead of relying on SKPaint.IsAntialias, create an SKFont and set its Edging property directly to SKFontEdging.Alias, then use the DrawText overload that accepts an SKFont parameter.

proposals.title: Fix updateFontEdging propagation
proposals.description: Debug the chain from SkCompatPaint::updateFontEdging() through text blob creation to find where font edging is lost. Likely requires stepping through native code to verify the edging value at each stage.

resolution.recommendedReason: Confirmed working by commenter jswolf19. Trivial code change and the SKFont API is the modern recommended approach (SKPaint text properties are deprecated).

actionability.reason: Real bug with complete repro, screenshots, and community confirmation in 3.119.0. Workaround exists but the underlying issue in the paint-to-font edging bridge needs debugging.

actions.description: Apply bug and area labels; add regression tenet
Acknowledge bug, provide workaround using SKFont.Edging directly
Link to #1996 (same IsAntialias bug on Linux)
Link to PR #1802 (the original fix for IsAntialias → Edging bridge)
